description: Commentary topics for Media Coach. Prompts use Kevin's direct, technically-grounded voice. Each topic now includes severity (1-5) for priority-based interruption and eventExposition for event-only display mode. Driver name placeholders: {ahead}, {behind}. Event exposition uses {value} for the primary trigger's current telemetry value.

topics.title: Big Save
topics.eventExposition: Snap oversteer — yaw rate hit {value} rad/s, full correction needed
topics.description: Extreme yaw event — genuine snap oversteer or spin attempt.

triggers.context: True snap oversteer — yaw rate well beyond normal cornering

commentaryPrompts: The rear came all the way round. That's the car communicating exactly what happens when you push past the limit — feel that in real time.
Massive snap right there. The difference between catching it and not is all in how much the wheel is actually talking to you.
That's the yaw limit of this car in real conditions. Talk the viewer through what you felt and what saved it.
Full rotation. That's not a controlled slide — that's the car telling you it's done with the corner.
Caught it, barely. The physics of that save are worth explaining — left foot, hands, instinct.

topics.title: Wall / Barrier Contact
topics.eventExposition: Hard impact — {value}G vertical spike, barrier or car contact
topics.description: Heavy impact — car hit a barrier or another vehicle at significant speed.

triggers.context: Very large vertical spike — hard contact with barrier, wall, or another car

commentaryPrompts: That's the wall. The race just changed in that impact — real damage to time, maybe to the car.
Contact with the barrier. That's a moment that resets everything — talk about what it costs you from here.
Hard impact. You feel that through the entire car. This is where the session either continues or it doesn't.
Into the barrier. It happened fast and the consequences are going to be felt for the rest of this stint.
That's a real hit. The sim is telling you exactly what that does to a car — let the viewer hear your reaction.

topics.title: Off Track
topics.eventExposition: Off track — vertical G spiked to {value}, left the racing surface
topics.description: Car left the track surface — run-off, gravel, grass.
commentaryPrompts: Off track, and that's entirely on me. The car went exactly where I pointed it.
Into the run-off. That's lap time I'm not getting back and honestly I deserve that.
Off the circuit. What's interesting is what the car does out here — totally different surface, totally different feedback.
Gone wide and off. That's what happens when confidence outruns grip.
Out of bounds. Self-inflicted. Moving on with the appropriate amount of embarrassment.

topics.title: Kerb / Curb Hit
topics.eventExposition: Hard kerb contact — {value}G vertical spike over the apex kerb
topics.description: Moderate vertical spike from hitting a kerb hard.

triggers.context: Solid kerb hit or bump — meaningful impact but not a crash

commentaryPrompts: Hit that kerb hard. You can feel it trying to unsettle the car — whether to use the kerbs here is a real setup decision.
Big impact over the apex kerb. The car reacted to that and you can feel why that's a risk in the sim.
That kerb grabbed the car. There's a reason some of these are controversial — they punish you for getting it slightly wrong.
Rode over that kerb and felt it everywhere. That's the sim doing its job — communicating what happens when you take that risk.

topics.title: ABS Activation
topics.eventExposition: ABS fired under braking — system intervened at the lock-up threshold
topics.description: ABS system fired — brakes locked before the system intervened.
commentaryPrompts: The ABS fired there. You can feel it working — the difference between that and locking up is what separates a controlled stop from a slide.
ABS active on that braking zone. Whether you rely on it or fight against it says a lot about how you're driving this car.
The system intervened under braking. That's the threshold — braked slightly past it and the electronics took over.
ABS on. In real life that pulse through the pedal is constant information. In the sim it's the same principle.

topics.title: Traction Control Cut
topics.eventExposition: TC cut power on corner exit — rear wheels breaking traction
topics.description: TC cut power on corner exit — rear sliding under throttle.
commentaryPrompts: TC cutting in on exit. The system is working and it's costing me a small amount of traction — it's a tradeoff I made in the setup.
Traction control fired. The rear wants to go and the computer says not yet — this is the balance between driver and electronics.
TC intervention on exit. You can feel it choking the throttle application — the question is whether the setting is right for this corner.
The system stepped in there. Running TC means you're trading raw exit speed for consistency — depends entirely on the circuit.

topics.title: Close Racing
topics.eventExposition: Wheel-to-wheel — car within 0.8% track distance, door-to-door racing
topics.description: Running wheel-to-wheel or within close proximity of another car.

triggers.context: Another car within 0.8% of track distance — genuinely close

commentaryPrompts: There's a car right there. Every input matters more when you're this close — one mistake and it's both of you in the barrier.
Door to door. This is the real test — how precise can you be when someone's watching every move you make?
Running this close in a race is genuinely intense. The margins in the sim match what this would feel like in real life.
That gap is nothing. Real wheel-to-wheel racing — the kind of moment that's worth being on stream for.

topics.title: Position Gained
topics.eventExposition: Overtake — passed {ahead}, moving up to P{value}
topics.description: Player moved up a position — made a clean pass.

triggers.context: Player moved up a race position

commentaryPrompts: Past {ahead}. That's a position gained and every one counts in this race.
Made the move on {ahead}. Clean pass, well-timed — that's real on-track progress.
Got past {ahead} and now it's about not making a mistake in the next few corners.
Position gained on {ahead}. The gap ahead is what matters now — build the margin.

topics.title: Position Lost
topics.eventExposition: Overtaken — {behind} got past, dropped to P{value}
topics.description: Player dropped a position — was overtaken.
commentaryPrompts: {behind} got me. That's a position gone and I need to respond — not panic, respond.
Lost that position to {behind}. Credit to them — clean move. Now I need to close that gap down.
{behind} found a way through. It stings but it's part of racing — figure out where I gave them the opportunity.
Dropped a place to {behind}. This is where the race either falls apart or I dig in and fix it.

topics.title: Yellow Flag
topics.eventExposition: Yellow flag — caution deployed, incident on track
topics.description: Yellow flag went active on track.
commentaryPrompts: Yellow flag out. Something happened and the race has paused itself — this changes the calculation completely.
Caution. The field bunches up and everything we built in terms of gap just resets. That's racing.
Yellow. Depending on where this falls in the stint it either saves us or costs us the strategy.
Flag is out. This is a moment where patience matters more than pace — get through the caution cleanly.

topics.title: Debris on Track
topics.eventExposition: Debris flag — hazardous material reported on the circuit
topics.description: Debris flag shown — hazardous material on the circuit.
commentaryPrompts: Debris flag. Something's on the circuit and you're running blind to it — this is where caution is actually the fast option.
Debris on track. In a real car this is one of the most dangerous moments — the sim captures that uncertainty.
Debris flag showing. This is exactly the kind of situation where incident points get handed out for no reason — stay clean.
Something on the circuit. Debris changes the risk calculation on every lap until it's cleared.

topics.title: Race Start
topics.eventExposition: Race start — lights out, lap 1 underway from P{value}
topics.description: Race session has just started.
commentaryPrompts: We're racing. The work that went into this session — setup, qualifying, pace — gets tested right now.
Lights out. Every bit of preparation comes down to the next ninety seconds of first lap chaos.
Race start. The goal is simple: survive lap one, then race from there. Nothing clever, just clean.
Here we go. First corner is make or break — stay out of trouble and build from whatever we come out of it with.

topics.title: Formation / Pace Lap
topics.eventExposition: Formation lap — building to race start, P{value}, check setup one last time
topics.description: Race formation lap — lap zero before lights out.

triggers.context: Lap count is zero in race session

commentaryPrompts: Formation lap. Last chance to check the setup feels right and get focused for the start.
Pace lap. Running behind the leader, building temperature in the tyres and brakes for what's coming.
This is the moment before it all gets real. Get the car sorted, tyres warm, head in the right place.
Building to the start. The formation lap is where you dial in mentally for the first corner chaos.

topics.title: Personal Best Lap
topics.eventExposition: Personal best — new fastest lap set
topics.description: New personal best lap time just set.
commentaryPrompts: That's a personal best. The lap did what it needed to do — clean, committed, no mistakes where it mattered.
PB. That's the car working and the driving matching it — every sector contributed.
New personal best. That's the result of getting the setup right and then actually executing on it.
Personal best lap and it felt like one. The confidence is there — now build on it.

topics.title: Pit Lane Entry
topics.eventExposition: Pit entry — coming in for service on lap {value}
topics.description: Player entered pit lane.
commentaryPrompts: Into the pits. This call has been made — now it's about coming out in the right window.
Pitting. The tyre situation made this decision and now the race reshapes around where we emerge.
Pit lane. A good stop is part of the lap time — everything has to go right in these few seconds.
Coming in for service. This is where the strategy either worked or it didn't. We'll find out in thirty seconds.

topics.title: Maximum Cornering Load
topics.eventExposition: Peak lateral load — {value}G through this corner, near the grip limit
topics.description: Very high lateral g sustained through a fast corner.

triggers.context: Extreme lateral load — near the absolute grip limit in a fast corner

commentaryPrompts: Maximum lateral load through that corner. The grip level in this car is something you have to experience to understand.
The car is absolutely planted. That's the engineering talking — the grip at this speed is extraordinary.
Full cornering load. The force through the seat and the wheel in a corner like that is why this car exists.
That's what the car was built for. Peak lateral G and it just takes it — totally committed.

topics.title: FFB Torque Spike
topics.eventExposition: FFB spike — steering torque jumped by {value}Nm, major front-end load event
topics.description: Large sudden torque spike through the steering — major kerb or snap load.

triggers.context: Very large torque spike — hard kerb hit or sudden front load

commentaryPrompts: The wheel just kicked back hard. That's the FFB telling you exactly what the front tyre just experienced.
Massive torque spike through the steering there. That's direct communication from the car — the wheel is doing its job.
That snap through the wheel was significant. That's the value of good FFB — you feel it before you see the car move.
The wheel fought back on that. Everything that went through the contact patch just came straight to your hands.

topics.title: Low Fuel
topics.eventExposition: Low fuel — tank at {value}%, running close to empty this stint
topics.description: Fuel level critically low — approaching the end of the stint.
commentaryPrompts: Fuel is getting critical. Every lap from here is a calculation — how much can I push before the tank says no.
Running low. The car gets lighter but the risk gets higher — this is where the fuel strategy either holds or it doesn't.
Low fuel and the math is tight. Lift, coast, manage — the pace suffers but finishing matters more than lap times right now.
The tank is running out. This is the honest part of long-distance racing — you plan for it but it still feels close.

topics.title: Wet Conditions
topics.eventExposition: Wet track declared — conditions changing, grip levels dropping
topics.description: Track conditions declared wet or rain detected.
commentaryPrompts: It's wet. Every reference point changes, every threshold drops, and the car becomes a completely different puzzle.
Wet track. This is where the real driving skill separates people — smooth inputs, read the surface, find the grip where it exists.
Rain conditions. The sim handles this seriously — tyre temp, water distribution, grip levels all in play at once.
Wet. The braking points move, the apexes move, the whole circuit gets rewritten by water.

topics.title: Cold Track Conditions
topics.eventExposition: Cold track surface — {value}°C, low grip conditions, tyres building temperature
topics.description: Track temperature is very cold — grip is limited.
commentaryPrompts: Cold track and the grip is limited. The tyres need building and rushing them is how you lose it.
Track temperature is low. Everything feels a step down from normal — pace builds gradually if you're patient.
It's cold out there. The window for grip is narrow and the car feels different through every corner.
Cold conditions. The tyres come in slowly and you have to respect that or the session gets away from you.

topics.title: High Tyre Wear
topics.eventExposition: High tyre wear — front left at {value}% life remaining, grip is degrading
topics.description: Significant tyre wear — car behavior degrading.

triggers.context: Front left at 35% or less remaining life (65%+ worn)
Front right at 35% or less remaining life (65%+ worn)
Rear left at 35% or less remaining life (65%+ worn)

commentaryPrompts: The tyres are going. You can feel it in every corner — the grip is leaving and the car is telling you clearly.
High tyre wear and the car's character is changing. The setup that worked at the start of the stint doesn't work the same way now.
Degradation is real. Every lap the window shifts and you have to adapt the driving style to what the rubber can still do.
The tyres have given a lot this stint. Managing what's left to the stop is the whole job right now.

topics.title: Overheating Tyres
topics.eventExposition: Tyre overheating — front temps at {value}°F, past the operating window
topics.description: Tyre temperatures significantly over optimal range.

triggers.context: Front left tyre temperature critically high (>250°F)
Front right tyre temperature critically high (>250°F)

commentaryPrompts: Tyres are overheating. The grip is going away and there's a physical reason — the compound can't handle this temperature.
Too hot on the fronts. This is what thermal degradation actually means — the rubber is greasing and the car knows it.
Temperatures are past the window. Managing this is a real skill — back off, build it back, or accept the pace hit.
The tyre temps are through the roof and the car is sliding everywhere. This is the sim communicating real physics.

topics.title: Major Braking Zone
topics.eventExposition: Heavy braking — {value}G deceleration into this corner
topics.description: Extreme longitudinal deceleration — very heavy braking event.
commentaryPrompts: That is a serious braking zone. The deceleration forces in a corner like that are eye-watering in any car.
Maximum braking right there. From that speed to nothing in that distance — the engineering that makes that possible is remarkable.
Big braking event. The car is right on the limit of what's physically possible and you can feel every bit of it.
That stop was aggressive. The balance under braking tells you everything about where this car is set up.

topics.title: Hot Qualifying Lap
topics.eventExposition: Hot lap — running {value}s ahead of personal best in qualifying
topics.description: Running significantly ahead of personal best in qualifying.

triggers.context: Running 0.8s ahead of PB — very fast lap in progress

commentaryPrompts: This lap is coming together. The delta is positive everywhere — just don't overthink it in the final sector.
Hot lap in qualifying and the car feels locked in. This is the result of getting every setup decision right.
The delta says this is the lap. Stay with it, don't brake early, don't run wide — this is what qualifying is for.
Every sector is contributing to this. The lap is clean — if it holds, this is a result worth having.

topics.title: iRacing Incident Points
topics.eventExposition: Incident — iRacing added penalty points, now at {value}x total
topics.description: iRacing incident count just increased.
commentaryPrompts: Incident points. iRacing saw that and the Safety Rating is going to reflect it — deserved.
The incident counter went up. In iRacing that's permanent — every contact is recorded and it matters for your rating.
That's on the record now. The iRating system doesn't care about context — it logs the incident and moves on.
Another incident point. The SR implications are real and they don't go away quickly. Need to drive cleaner.

topics.title: ERS Battery Low
topics.eventExposition: ERS depleted — battery at {value}%, no hybrid boost available
topics.description: ERS battery nearly depleted — losing hybrid power.
commentaryPrompts: ERS is almost gone. The performance difference without the hybrid is immediately noticeable on every straight.
Battery depleted. From here to the next regen zone, everything comes from the internal combustion engine alone.
The hybrid is out. Managing these depletion windows is a core skill in these cars — it changes the car completely.
ERS empty and the car feels slower in a very specific way. That's the delta between hybrid and non-hybrid performance.

topics.title: High Track Temperature
topics.eventExposition: Track surface at {value}°C — high temps accelerating tyre degradation
topics.description: Track temperature very high — affects degradation and grip window.
commentaryPrompts: Track temperature is high and the tyres are working in a brutal environment. Degradation is accelerated in these conditions.
Hot track surface. The rubber goes down fast and the grip window is narrow — this is a proper tyre management challenge.
Temperatures like this change the whole race. The compounds peak early and fall off hard — every lap is a tradeoff.
The tarmac is baking. The grip is there early in the stint and then it goes away faster than you'd like.

topics.title: DRS Open
topics.eventExposition: DRS open — rear wing flap deployed, straight-line speed boost active
topics.description: DRS rear wing is open — straight-line speed advantage active.
commentaryPrompts: DRS is open. You feel the drag drop away and the car just accelerates differently — it's a real performance delta.
Rear wing open. The straight-line advantage is genuine and it changes the overtaking calculation on this circuit.
DRS zone active. Whether that's enough to make the move is the question — the physics are working in your favor right now.
The wing is open. That speed delta is real and the car knows it — this is where the pass either happens or it doesn't.

topics.title: Car On The Limit
topics.eventExposition: Car hooked up — sustained {value}G lateral load, perfectly balanced
topics.description: Sustained high lateral load — car working perfectly through a complex corner.

triggers.context: Sustained high lateral load — car is right at the grip limit

commentaryPrompts: The car is right at the limit through that section. When it's working this well, you stop thinking and start feeling.
That's a well-sorted car doing its job. The balance is exactly where you need it — rotation on demand, grip when you ask for it.
The setup is translating into lap time right there. That kind of trust in the car makes you drive differently.
Completely hooked up. The car is communicating everything and responding to everything — that's the feeling you're building the session around.

topics.title: Black Flag
topics.eventExposition: Black flag — disqualified from session
topics.description: Black flag received — session disqualification.
commentaryPrompts: That's the black flag. The session is done for me — this is the ultimate penalty in iRacing.
Black flag down. Whatever it was that earned this, it's over now. The race doesn't continue from here.
Disqualified. That flag means it's done — go to the garage and accept it.
Black flag and we're out. This is the consequence of whatever led to this point.

topics.title: Aggressive Gear Shift
topics.eventExposition: Aggressive shift — rapid gear change, multiple gears in one corner
topics.description: Rapid multi-gear downshift or upshift event.

triggers.context: More than 2 gears changed in single frame

commentaryPrompts: Quick downshift sequence there. Multiple gears in rapid succession — that's the aggression the corner demanded.
Stacked downshift and the car responded. Going from high gear to low quickly like that changes how the car rotates.
Aggressive gearing decision. The transmission is doing what it needs to do to set up for what's coming next.
Multi-gear shift and the engine response follows. That sequencing is part of how you manage the rotation into a complex corner.

topics.title: Long Stint Distance
topics.eventExposition: Long stint underway — {value} laps completed, management now critical
topics.description: Extended stint milestone — driver is deep into long-distance running.

triggers.context: More than 15 laps completed — long stint territory

commentaryPrompts: We're deep in the stint now. It's all about management from here — fuel, tyres, and patience.
This is the marathon part of the race. After fifteen laps it becomes about consistency and smart decisions.
Long stint mode. The car dynamics are different now with fuel load and tyre wear compounding every lap.
We're well into it now. The pace has to be sustainable and the strategy has to hold from here to the stop.

topics.title: Session Time Running Out
topics.eventExposition: Time running out — {value} seconds remaining, final laps of the session
topics.description: Session time remaining is critically low — final minutes active.
commentaryPrompts: Time's running out. Less than five minutes and every lap counts. The pace has to be maximum now.
The clock is winding down. This is the final push of the race and it's time to leave everything on the track.
Running out of time. Whatever position I have now, the gaps have to be managed through to the end.
The session is almost done. These final minutes are where positions are made and lost — it's go time.
